name: bsdutils
title: bsdutils
description:
basic utilities from 4.4BSD-Lite
This package contains the bare minimum of BSD utilities needed for a Debian
system: renice, script, scriptlive, scriptreplay and wall. The
remaining standard BSD utilities are provided by bsdextrautils.

commands.name: scriptlive
commands.description: re-run session typescripts, using timing information

commands.name: scriptreplay
commands.description: play back typescripts, using timing information

commands.name: wall
commands.description: write a message to all users

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use renice because basic utilities from 4.4BSD-Lite.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use renice as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `renice [-n|--priority|--relative] priority [-g|-p|-u] identifier...` as documented upstream for renice.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `renice +1 987 -u daemon root -p 32` as documented upstream for renice.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair renice with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `renice --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: renice - alter priority of running processes
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install util-linux (2.42.2-1). Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `renice [-n|--priority|--relative] priority [-g|-p|-u] identifier...`. Further invocations from upstream: `renice +1 987 -u daemon root -p 32`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: basic utilities from 4.4BSD-Lite.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: renice - alter priority of running processes.
